articles.title: Starbucks Surges Over 2.7% Amid Governance Shifts, Loyalty Program Overhaul, and Rallying Investor Sentiment
articles.summary: Starbucks (SBUX) shares surged over 2.7% following corporate updates including governance shifts and a revamped loyalty program, pushing the stock above its 200-day moving average. Despite mixed investor sentiment regarding the loyalty program and labor relations, analysts have adjusted their outlooks, with Guggenheim raising its target to $95. The article also details options trading opportunities for aggressive bulls looking to capitalize on potential price movements ahead of the April 10 expiration.
articles.text_to_analyze: Starbucks Surges Over 2.7% Amid Governance Shifts, Loyalty Program Overhaul, and Rallying Investor Sentiment. Starbucks (SBUX) shares surged over 2.7% following corporate updates including governance shifts and a revamped loyalty program, pushing the stock above its 200-day moving average. Despite mixed investor sentiment regarding the loyalty program and labor relations, analysts have adjusted their outlooks, with Guggenheim raising its target to $95. The article also details options trading opportunities for aggressive bulls looking to capitalize on potential price movements ahead of the April 10 expiration.

articles.title: [PX14A6G] STARBUCKS CORP SEC Filing
articles.summary: This SEC filing (Form PX14A6G) by Starbucks Corporation details an exempt solicitation by the Comptroller of the City of New York on behalf of several New York City pension funds. The solicitation urges Starbucks shareholders to vote against the re-election of Board Directors Jørgen Vig Knudstorp and Beth Ford due to ongoing labor issues and a rollback of independent board oversight since 2021. The filing includes texts of social media posts by the NYC Comptroller disseminating this recommendation.
